careers.title: Technical Project & Team Lead
careers.overview:
Xponent, established in 2003 and operating from Chattogram, Bangladesh, builds custom software, AI-powered products, and SaaS platforms — including our own products in the digital marketing space. We are a growing technology company with a strong focus on product quality, engineering excellence, and long-term client success.

As we grow, we need someone to own the space between the founder and the development team. This is not a coding role. This is a leadership and delivery role. You will manage projects, lead developers, and ensure that what gets built is the right thing — delivered on time, with quality, and with clear communication throughout.
careers.callout: This role is not for someone looking for a short-term contract or a stepping stone. We are building a team for the long term. We are looking for someone who wants to grow here — into senior leadership — and build a career, not just fill a seat. If you are serious about that kind of trajectory, we want to hear from you.
responsibilities: Take full ownership of project delivery — from kickoff to production
Keep a team of 3–5 developers organised, focused, and accountable
Communicate clearly between the founder and the dev team
Translate business needs into technical direction and vice versa
Spot problems early — timeline risks, blockers, misalignment — and act before they escalate
Run projects with structure and discipline without creating unnecessary process
Hold the team to quality and reliability standards — not just deadlines
Work with sincerity, diligence, and full professional accountability
whatYouWillOwn: Day to day management of development teams across active projects
Project timelines, milestones, and delivery accountability
Communication between founder and development team
GitHub Projects for task tracking and progress visibility
Identifying and resolving blockers before they impact delivery
Ensuring quality and reliability standards before release
Running team check-ins and keeping everyone aligned
requirements: 4–5 years in a technical project management or team lead role
Strong enough technical background to communicate with developers
Experience managing small development teams of 3–5 people
Strong team leadership and people management skills
Excellent organisational, analytical, and communication skills
Ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical and team challenges efficiently
Comfortable working directly with a founder in a fast-moving environment
Based in Chattogram or willing to relocate
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field preferred
niceToHave: Experience in a product-focused or agency environment
Familiarity with agile or kanban delivery methodologies
Prior experience in a founder-led or fast-moving startup
successMetrics: Projects delivered on time and within scope
Team stays focused, aligned, and unblocked
Founder has full visibility into project status at all times
Quality and reliability standards met before every release
Escalations handled early — problems don't reach the founder as surprises
whyJoin: Own the bridge between leadership and the development team
Work directly with the founder in a high-trust, high-impact role
Build and lead a team for the long term
Grow into senior leadership in a fast-moving technology company
On-site environment with a serious, committed team

careers.title: Head of Growth — Founding Hire
careers.experience: Open — trajectory matters more than years
careers.overview:
If you're a digital marketer who has sensed that the job is changing under your feet — that AI is quietly eating the parts you used to get paid for — and you've been actively pushing yourself to become something new, read on.

We're not hiring for yesterday's digital marketing role. We're hiring for what it's becoming: a growth operator who runs strategy and judgment, and directs a team of humans and AI agents to do the rest.

You don't need a perfect CV. You need the right trajectory.

We're building a growing portfolio of online products and SaaS tools. Some are already live with paying customers; others are on their way to becoming AI-operable in the near term. What we don't yet have is a real growth function.

That's the seat you'd take.

This is a founding role. You'll set the growth strategy, build the stack, decide what humans do, what AI agents do, and what we outsource. You'll report directly to the founder and have real ownership — not "marketing manager" scope, but "you own whether our products grow" scope.
careers.callout: This is a founding role with real scope and real ownership. We don't care whether you've done SaaS growth before. We care whether you're hungry, curious, and willing to build something new instead of clinging to a playbook that's getting outdated. Experience level is open — if you're a sharp mid-level digital marketer with the right trajectory, we want to hear from you. If you're senior and already doing this, even better.
responsibilities: Own the funnel across products — acquisition, activation, conversion, retention, expansion. Define what we measure, set the targets, move them
Build positioning and narrative for each product — who it's for, why it wins, what we say, what we don't. This is the human work that cannot be handed off
Talk to customers constantly — sit in on support, read tickets, hop on calls. Form a real point of view on what users actually need
Design and run experiments — decide what's worth testing, read results honestly, kill what doesn't work

Architect a human + AI team — direct a small team of humans plus an expanding stack of AI agents handling content production, ad variants, lifecycle email, competitor tracking, reporting, lead enrichment, and outreach
Run channels and partners — paid, SEO, content, community, partnerships. Decide where to play, bring in contractors when needed, keep the whole thing coherent
Tie marketing to revenue — work closely with product and the founder to connect what you do to business outcomes that matter
whatYouWillOwn: Growth strategy across a portfolio of products
requirements: You think clearly and write well — everything downstream flows from how you frame things
You have taste — you can look at an AI-generated output and know when it's generic garbage vs. when it's good
You're comfortable with ambiguity and early-stage chaos
You understand numbers — funnel math, conversion rates, basic unit economics. Or you're eager to learn
You see AI as a team member, not a tool to help write captions faster
You're ready to own outcomes, not just activities
whoYouAre: Started using ChatGPT, Claude, or similar tools seriously in your own work
Noticed that a lot of what you were taught to do can now be done in minutes by a prompt
Begun asking yourself what the next version of this job looks like
Started experimenting — maybe building workflows, trying new tools, reading about AI agents, thinking about SaaS and product growth
niceToHave: Experience in SaaS or product-led growth
Background in running paid acquisition or SEO at scale
Familiarity with AI agent workflows and automation tools
Experience managing contractors or small teams
Understanding of unit economics and subscription metrics
successMetrics: Products are growing — measurable improvement in acquisition, activation, and retention
A working growth stack exists — humans, AI agents, and tools operating as a system
Positioning is clear and differentiated across products
Experiments are running regularly with honest reads on results
The founder has full visibility into what's working and what's not
Revenue impact is trackable and growing
whyJoin: A founding seat with real scope, real ownership, and direct work with the founder
A portfolio of products to grow, not a single campaign to babysit
A company that takes AI-native operation seriously — not as a buzzword, but as how we build
A steep learning curve with full backing — Claude and similar tools are part of how we work, every day
A small, sharp in-office team in Chattogram
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Instead, send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: A short note on a marketing or growth problem you've worked on — what was the situation, what did you do, what happened, and what would you do differently today?
One concrete example of how you've used AI to do something meaningfully better or faster than you would have done it the traditional way. Be specific — which tool, what setup, what outcome.
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Marketing Associate — Generalist
careers.overview:
You're early in your marketing career, or you've spent a few years doing a bit of everything and you like it that way. You're hungry, fast, curious, and you don't want to specialize into a narrow box just yet. You've started using AI tools seriously — not to cheat, but because you realized they make you 3x faster at the boring parts so you can spend time on the interesting parts.

If that's you, this role is the best generalist seat we have to offer.

The Head of Growth sets strategy. The specialists (content, design, paid, success, community) go deep in their lanes. You're the person in the middle who makes things move.

You'll be the right hand to the Head of Growth — running coordination, executing across whatever the week demands, directing AI agents for day-to-day production, and keeping the whole operation from dropping balls.

This is a role where you learn more in 6 months than most people do in 2 years. It's also a role where if you want to eventually specialize (into content, paid, product marketing, whatever) you'll have had exposure to all of it.
responsibilities: Execute across whatever the funnel needs this week — briefs, pages, emails, social, copy, experiments, reports
Direct AI agents for production work: drafts, research, competitor monitoring, enrichment, variants
Own the calendar, the project tracker, the "who's doing what by when"
Run quality-check on AI outputs before they go live — taste is your job, not just speed
Pull together weekly reporting across channels
Jump into customer conversations, read support tickets, bring the team insights from the field
Help hire and onboard the next person behind you
requirements: 1–3 years in marketing (agency, in-house, freelance — doesn't matter) or a sharp fresh grad who has already been doing real work on the side
You write clearly — you think in outlines and bullets when you need to, prose when it matters
You've already been using ChatGPT, Claude, or similar as part of your actual workflow — not just experimenting
You notice when AI output sounds generic and you know how to fix it
You like owning things end-to-end, even when the things are small
You're comfortable with messy, fast, multi-product environments
niceToHave: Experience in SaaS or product marketing
Familiarity with project management tools and workflows
Background in content, social, or email marketing
Experience directing AI agents or automation workflows
successMetrics: The growth team runs smoothly — nothing gets dropped
AI agents are producing on-brand output under your direction
Weekly reporting is clear, honest, and on time
Cross-functional execution is fast and high quality
The Head of Growth can focus on strategy because you own execution
whyJoin: The fastest generalist learning curve in marketing — exposure to every channel and function
Right hand to the Head of Growth with direct access to the founder
A founding team seat with real ownership, not junior busywork
AI-native work environment where you'll build skills most marketers won't have for years
A clear path to specialize once you find what you love
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: A short note on something you've shipped or built — could be a campaign, a project, a side hustle, a content piece, anything. What was it, what did you do, what happened?
One concrete example of how you use AI in your work. Which tool, what setup, what outcome.
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Content Lead — SEO + Content Operations
careers.overview:
You're an SEO or content specialist who has been watching the ground shift. You've seen the "write 20 blog posts a month" model get commoditized overnight by AI. You've read the debates about whether SEO is dead (it isn't, but the old playbook is). And somewhere along the way, you've made a decision: you're not going to be the person AI replaces. You're going to be the person who directs AI to do what used to take a team of 10.

If that's you, this is a role where your instincts and your AI fluency both matter — and where you can build something serious.

Content and SEO are the most obvious place where AI has changed the economics. What took a team now takes one sharp operator with the right stack. But AI alone produces generic, low-trust, search-invisible garbage. The work is in the direction: what to write, why, for whom, how to make it actually rank and actually convert.

You'll own content and SEO end-to-end across our online products. You'll set the topical strategy, build the content engine (humans + AI), and own organic as a growth channel. You'll also work closely with the Head of Growth on positioning — because content is where positioning meets traffic.
responsibilities: Own organic search and content as a growth channel — from strategy down to published output
Build the topical map for each product: what we rank for, what we own, what we don't bother with
Run the content engine: briefs, drafts, editing, publishing, internal linking, updates
Architect the human + AI workflow — what AI drafts, what humans write, what humans gate, what gets killed
Own editorial judgment and quality bar — nothing generic ships
Build and manage link-building and authority-growth plays
Track what's working — rankings, traffic, conversions — and iterate based on results, not vanity
Collaborate with design and performance on content that supports paid and lifecycle
requirements: You've done SEO or content marketing for at least a year or two and can talk about rankings, intent, SERPs, internal linking, and content quality with actual opinions
You've already rebuilt your workflow around AI — you've probably prompted, iterated, built templates, maybe tried agent setups
You write well yourself, and you know what bad writing looks like
You understand that "AI content" isn't a strategy — quality and relevance are. AI is the factory, you're the editor-in-chief
You're curious about what's changing in search (AI Overviews, Perplexity, LLM-driven discovery) and thinking about what's next
You can read data and make calls, not just list metrics in a dashboard
niceToHave: Experience with programmatic or scaled content operations
Background in SaaS or product-led content
Familiarity with AI agent workflows for content production
Link-building or digital PR experience
Understanding of conversion copywriting
successMetrics: Organic traffic growing across products
Content engine running — consistent, high-quality output at scale
Rankings improving on target topics
Content contributing to conversions, not just pageviews
Editorial quality bar is clear and enforced
AI workflow is documented and repeatable
whyJoin: Own content and SEO across a portfolio of products — not one blog on one product
Build the content engine from scratch with full AI integration
Direct access to the Head of Growth and founder
A company that understands AI-native content is the future, not a shortcut
A small, sharp in-office team in Chattogram
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: A piece of writing you're proud of — blog post, ranked article, landing page, newsletter, your pick. Include the URL and tell us why you're proud of it.
One concrete example of how you've used AI in content or SEO work. What was the setup, what did you produce, what would've been different without AI?
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Performance Marketer — Paid + Analytics
careers.overview:
You've been running paid campaigns — Meta, Google, maybe LinkedIn or TikTok — for a while. You've watched the platforms swallow more and more of the work: automated bidding, Advantage+, Performance Max. At first it felt threatening; now you understand it for what it is. The algorithms handle the mechanics. Your job is to feed them the right inputs (audiences, creative, signals, offers) and read what they tell you back.

You've also figured out that analytics and attribution are now the leverage points. Anyone can launch a campaign. Very few people can tell you honestly whether it's working.

As our products scale, paid will matter — but only if we can measure it honestly and iterate fast. We don't want someone who "runs ads." We want someone who owns paid as a real growth channel and who can tie it back to actual business outcomes.

You'll own paid across channels, own the analytics layer, and partner closely with design and content on the creative that feeds it.
responsibilities: Own paid channels end-to-end: strategy, budgets, targeting, creative briefs, optimization, reporting
Run the creative testing engine — direct AI for variant production, work with the designer on concepts that matter, kill what doesn't work
Own the analytics and attribution setup — what we track, how we track it, what we trust, what we don't
Build the reporting that tells the team (and the founder) what's actually happening, not what looks good on a slide
Work on landing pages and conversion rate optimization with the Head of Growth and designer
Partner with Content and Community on lifecycle + retargeting flows
Keep a running view of unit economics — CAC, payback, LTV — and flag when things shift
requirements: 1–3+ years running paid campaigns with real budget responsibility (even small budgets count if you owned the results)
Comfortable with numbers and analytics — GA4, Meta Ads Manager, spreadsheets, basic SQL is a bonus but not required
You've already started using AI for creative variants, ad copy, audience research, or analytics — and you can show us how
You treat reporting as a thinking exercise, not a checkbox — you'd rather show one honest insight than ten pretty charts
You understand that creative is the biggest lever in paid today — and you know how to judge whether an ad is any good
You're curious about what's changing in measurement (iOS changes, cookie deprecation, server-side tracking, incrementality testing)
niceToHave: Experience with Meta and Google Ads at scale
Background in analytics or data — SQL, GA4, Mixpanel, Amplitude
Understanding of conversion rate optimization
Experience with AI-generated creative at scale
Familiarity with attribution challenges in a post-cookie world
successMetrics: Paid channels are profitable and scaling — CAC, ROAS, and payback are tracked and improving
Creative testing is running continuously with clear learnings
Analytics and attribution are set up honestly — the team trusts the numbers
Reporting tells the story, not just the stats
Unit economics are visible and flagged early when they shift
Landing page conversion rates are improving through testing
whyJoin: Own paid and analytics across a portfolio of products, not a single ad account
Work with a designer and content team who understand creative matters
AI-native workflow — use AI for variants, research, and reporting at scale
Direct access to the Head of Growth and founder
A small, sharp in-office team in Chattogram
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: A short write-up of a campaign you ran — budget, setup, what you tested, what you learned, what the outcome was. If it flopped, tell us what you'd do differently.
One concrete example of how you've used AI in paid or analytics work. Which tool, what setup, what outcome.
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Designer — Brand + Product Marketing
careers.overview:
You're a designer who has watched AI tools go from novelty to essential in about two years. You've probably used Midjourney, tried Figma's AI features, maybe played with v0 or similar. You've noticed that AI is great at "an image" and still pretty bad at "a coherent brand."

That gap is where your job lives. Taste, systems thinking, and knowing when a computer-generated output is "close enough" vs. "no, this is off" — that's the work.

We're building a portfolio of products that all need to feel like ours. Not generic. Not AI-slop. Not agency-cookie-cutter. That only happens if one person is holding the thread — building the brand systems, setting the visual standards, and making sure every touchpoint (landing pages, ads, social, product screens, email) feels intentional.

You'll own brand and marketing design across our products. You'll use AI aggressively for scale and iteration, but you'll be the taste layer that keeps everything from drifting into sameness.
responsibilities: Build and maintain brand systems for each product — type, color, spacing, voice, visual language
Design landing pages, ads, social creative, email templates, pitch decks — with the whole team, not in isolation
Direct AI tools (Midjourney, Figma AI, Canva AI, etc.) for variants, iteration, and production work
Partner with Performance on ad creative that actually converts
Partner with Content on visual storytelling and editorial design
Build templates and systems so the rest of the team (and AI agents) can produce on-brand output without you being a bottleneck
Own the quality bar for anything that ships with a visual in it
requirements: 2+ years of design experience — brand, marketing, product, or a mix
A portfolio that shows taste, not just skill — you can tell good work from bad, and you can explain why
Already using AI tools in your actual workflow — you've formed opinions about what they're good at and where they fall short
Comfortable in Figma as a given — comfortable with speed over perfection when it matters
You care about the whole system, not just the artifact — you think about how templates, components, and patterns compound
You can take input from non-designers without being defensive, and you can push back when something is wrong
niceToHave: Experience building brand systems or design systems from scratch
Background in SaaS or product marketing design
Motion design or video editing skills
Familiarity with front-end implementation (CSS, Tailwind, component libraries)
Experience with AI image generation workflows at scale
successMetrics: Products have a coherent, intentional visual identity — not generic, not AI-slop
Templates and systems exist so the team can produce on-brand output without bottlenecks
Ad and landing page creative is converting — not just looking pretty
AI tools are integrated into the design workflow for speed without sacrificing quality
The quality bar is clear and maintained across all touchpoints
Design turnaround is fast — the team isn't waiting on you
whyJoin: Own brand and design across a portfolio of products — build something coherent from scratch
Work with a growth team that understands design matters, not just "make it pretty"
AI-native workflow — use AI aggressively for scale, be the taste layer that keeps it real
Direct access to the Head of Growth and founder
A small, sharp in-office team in Chattogram
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: Your portfolio — link or PDF. Tell us which 2–3 pieces you're most proud of and why.
One concrete example of how you've used AI in design work. Which tool, what for, what the outcome was, and what you had to fix about the AI output.
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Customer Success — Activation & Retention
careers.experience: 1–3 years in support or customer success
careers.overview:
You've worked in support, customer success, or account management. You're good with people, patient with confused users, and genuinely curious about why humans behave the way they do. Somewhere along the way, you realized two things: first, that the "success" part of customer success is really about product and growth — getting people to real value fast. Second, that AI is going to handle a lot of the reactive support work that used to eat your day, freeing you to do the strategic part you actually like.

This role is designed for exactly that shift.

Signup is the beginning, not the goal. Our growth depends on whether users actually get value from our products — whether they activate, stick, and eventually pay more. That work lives in the gap between marketing and product, and right now nobody owns it.

You will.

You'll own the post-signup experience, drive activation, reduce churn, and — critically — bring the rest of the team a constant stream of insight from real users. Everyone else on the team will be smarter because you're talking to customers every day.
responsibilities: Own the user journey from signup through first value, onboarding, and ongoing engagement
Design and run onboarding flows, lifecycle emails, and in-product nudges (with the team, AI-assisted for scale)
Talk to users constantly — calls, surveys, support tickets, community posts — and turn that into insight
Run the support function: set up AI-first triage (Claude, chatbots, knowledge bases) and handle the hard cases yourself
Build and maintain help docs, FAQs, onboarding content — AI drafts, your judgment gates
Track activation rates, retention curves, churn signals — flag when something breaks, propose what to fix
Partner with product on feedback loops — what users ask for, what confuses them, where they drop off
Be the team's voice-of-the-customer — not in a corporate sense but in a real one
requirements: 1–3 years in customer support, customer success, account management, or similar — SaaS experience is a plus but not required
You genuinely like helping people, and you get impatient when the product makes their life harder than it needs to be
You're comfortable writing — emails, help docs, in-app copy — because a lot of this job is written communication
You've started using AI in your workflow (drafting responses, summarizing tickets, building help content) and you can show us how
You're analytical enough to read retention data and spot patterns, or eager to learn
You don't see AI-first support as a threat — you see it as a way to finally stop answering the same question 50 times a day and focus on the conversations that actually matter
niceToHave: Experience with SaaS onboarding or activation work
Familiarity with support tools (Intercom, Zendesk, Crisp, etc.)
Background in building help centers or knowledge bases
Experience with lifecycle email or in-app messaging
Understanding of retention metrics and cohort analysis
successMetrics: Activation rates improving — more users reaching first value faster
Churn decreasing — users who activate are staying and expanding
AI-first support is running — common questions handled automatically, you handle the hard ones
The team has a constant stream of user insight — what's working, what's breaking, what's confusing
Help docs and onboarding content are high quality and up to date
Users feel heard — NPS, CSAT, or qualitative signals are positive and improving
whyJoin: Own the entire post-signup experience — activation, retention, and the voice of the customer
Build AI-first support from scratch instead of inheriting a legacy system
Direct access to the Head of Growth and founder
Your insights directly shape product and growth decisions
A small, sharp in-office team in Chattogram
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: A short note about a tough customer situation you handled — what was the situation, what did you do, what happened?
One concrete example of how you've used AI in customer-facing work. Which tool, what setup, what outcome.
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Community & Social Lead
careers.overview:
You've been running social accounts, managing communities, or doing content-creator-adjacent work for a while. You know the grind — daily posts, engagement, DMs, the endless feed of everyone's takes. And you've started noticing something: AI can now produce "a LinkedIn post" in 10 seconds, but AI cannot build an audience, hold a voice, or know which comment to reply to and how.

The human parts of this job just became the whole job. That suits you fine.

In a world where AI floods every feed with decent-but-forgettable content, what cuts through is voice, point of view, and actual relationships. That's what we want to build — across LinkedIn, X, YouTube, newsletter, whatever fits our products and our audience.

You'll own community and social across our products. You'll build audience, hold brand voice, engage real humans, and use AI as scaffolding rather than as a replacement for thought.
responsibilities: Own social and community strategy across channels — decide where to play, why, and what we're trying to do in each
Hold and evolve brand voice — every post should sound like us, not like every other SaaS feed
Produce and publish — mix of your own writing, AI-drafted content you direct and polish, and team contributions you coordinate
Run the posting engine: calendar, variants, scheduling, reposts, repurposing across formats
Engage — reply to comments, hop into relevant conversations, build relationships with people who matter in our space
Build and nurture community spaces (Slack, Discord, subreddit, newsletter comments, wherever our users gather)
Partner with Content on turning long-form into social, and with Customer Success on turning user stories into posts
Track what's working — not vanity metrics, but the ones that tie to growth
requirements: 1–3 years running social accounts, community, or creator-adjacent work (in-house, agency, or your own projects)
A strong writing voice, and you can flex it across serious and playful
You've already been using AI for drafts, variants, repurposing — and you know the difference between AI-assisted good content and AI-generated slop
You understand that the job isn't "post 10 times a week," it's "build an audience that trusts us" — those are very different things
You're comfortable being in public as part of the brand — replying, engaging, sometimes being the face
You're up to date on what's working on each platform right now — and humble enough to change your mind when things shift
niceToHave: Experience building a community from scratch
Background in SaaS or tech social media
Video or short-form content creation skills
Experience with social listening and analytics tools
A personal following or content portfolio that shows your voice
successMetrics: Audience is growing on key channels — followers, subscribers, community members
Engagement is real — conversations, not just likes. People reply, share, and come back
Brand voice is consistent and recognizable across channels
Community spaces are active and providing real value to users
Social is contributing to growth — referral traffic, signups, brand awareness that ties to pipeline
AI is integrated into the workflow for speed without sacrificing voice
whyJoin: Own community and social across a portfolio of products — build real audience, not just manage a feed
Work with a content team and growth team that understand voice matters
AI-native workflow — use AI for scaffolding, be the voice that makes it human
Direct access to the Head of Growth and founder
A small, sharp in-office team in Chattogram
careers.applicationNote: Skip cover letters. Send us these three things — they're what we actually read:
applicationSteps: Link us to a social account, community, or piece of content you've built or helped build. Tell us what you did and what the outcome was.
One concrete example of how you use AI in social or community work — what you use it for, what you don't, and why.
Your CV or LinkedIn.

careers.title: Head of Marketing & Growth
careers.overview: We are looking for a Head of Marketing & Growth to lead the expansion of Xponent's technology services. This role is responsible for building our lead generation engine, improving client acquisition, and developing strategic partnerships with businesses and enterprises. This is not a social media role. This is a growth leadership role focused on revenue, client acquisition, and market expansion.
responsibilities: Develop and execute the marketing roadmap for Xponent's services and products
Build a predictable lead generation system using content, webinars, events, and campaigns
Design and improve the sales funnel from lead to closed deal
Work closely with the sales team to improve conversion rates
Develop partnerships with technology vendors, agencies, and enterprise clients
Define messaging and positioning for custom software, AI automation, and digital platform services
Track marketing performance and optimize campaigns
Manage marketing and sales execution team
requirements: 3–7 years experience in marketing, growth, or business development
Experience in B2B technology, SaaS, or software services marketing preferred
Strong understanding of marketing funnels and conversion optimization
Ability to build systems, not just campaigns
Comfortable working with CRM and marketing automation tools
Strong communication and leadership skills
niceToHave: Experience marketing technology services or software development
Startup or agency experience
Growth marketing background
Partnership development experience
Understanding of AI, automation, or enterprise software
successMetrics: Qualified leads generated
Lead-to-client conversion rate
Cost per lead and cost per acquisition
Revenue growth
Partnership deals closed
whyJoin: Work directly with founders building cutting-edge technology solutions
Opportunity to position a growing technology company in the market
Own the growth strategy instead of executing small tasks
Fast learning startup environment
Impact real businesses through digital transformation

careers.title: Sales Executive
careers.overview: We are hiring Sales Executives to help convert interested businesses into Xponent clients through consultation, needs analysis, and solution presentation. This role focuses on understanding client challenges, communicating our technology capabilities, and closing B2B service deals.

Understand business challenges and identify technology solutions
Explain Xponent's services clearly to potential clients
Schedule discovery and consultation sessions
Present proposals and service packages
Follow up with interested leads through the sales cycle
Maintain CRM records and pipeline management
Collaborate with technical team on solution scoping
Help improve sales scripts and processes based on feedback

Strong communication and presentation skills
Comfortable speaking with business owners and decision-makers
Understanding of technology services or willingness to learn quickly
Organized and disciplined approach to sales process
Goal-oriented mindset

careers.title: Technology Content Writer
careers.overview: We are looking for a Technology Content Writer to help build authority for Xponent through high-quality educational and thought leadership content. This role focuses on writing content that attracts business leaders, CTOs, product managers, and decision-makers looking for technology solutions.

Research technology trends and business challenges
Create educational content around software development, AI, and business systems

careers.title: Senior Product Engineer (Backend / NodeJS)
careers.overview: We are looking for a Senior Product Engineer to help architect and build scalable software platforms, AI-powered systems, and digital business solutions for our clients. This role is for engineers who think about systems, not just endpoints. You will work closely with the founders and clients to design backend architecture, improve reliability, and guide development teams.

Architect APIs and microservices communication
Build AI-powered automation systems and integrations
Improve system reliability, performance, and security

Support Dockerized deployments and CI/CD pipelines
Guide and mentor junior developers
Review code and enforce best practices

Collaborate on product architecture decisions with clients
Participate in technical discovery and solution design
whatYouWillOwn: Code quality standards and technical direction

Experience building production SaaS products or enterprise systems
Strong understanding of backend architecture and system design
Experience with APIs, databases, queues, and async systems

Strong Git workflow understanding

careers.title: Full Stack JavaScript Developer
careers.overview:
Xponent builds custom software, AI-powered products, and SaaS platforms — including our own products in the digital marketing space. If you are a developer who wants to work on real SaaS products with real users, alongside client projects across different industries, this is that kind of role.

We are looking for a Full Stack JavaScript Developer who operates beyond the traditional coding role — someone who leads delivery, not just executes it.

AI now handles the bulk of code generation. Your role is to use that capability fully and responsibly — as a builder, a manager, and an owner. As a builder, you direct AI tools to design and develop full-stack features. As a manager, you own the quality of that output — reviewing, testing, debugging, and integrating until it meets production standards. As an owner, you take full responsibility for what ships: its reliability, its correctness, and how it fits within the broader system.

This means your time is spent less on writing individual lines of code and more on understanding what needs to be built, making sound decisions, driving delivery forward, and communicating clearly with the team.
careers.callout: This role is not for someone looking for a short-term contract or a stepping stone. We are building a team for the long term. We are looking for someone who wants to grow here — into senior and eventually leadership positions — and build a career, not just fill a seat. If you are serious about that kind of trajectory, we want to hear from you.
responsibilities: Take features from requirement to production with full ownership
Use AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude, Copilot, etc.) to build full-stack features across Next.js and Node.js
Review, test, and iterate on AI-generated code until it meets production standards
Debug issues across the stack — whether in AI output, your own work, or existing systems
Understand the requirements before building — ask questions, clarify scope, flag concerns early
Maintain and improve existing client products and internal systems
Communicate progress, blockers, and decisions clearly and proactively with the team
Participate in technical discussions and contribute to solution decisions
whatYouWillOwn: End-to-end delivery of features on live client products
Production reliability and correctness of what you ship
Code quality and integration within your areas of the codebase
Technical decisions within your features — approach, trade-offs, and execution
requirements: 2–4 years of hands-on experience building real full-stack JavaScript products
Strong proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript
Experience with Next.js — this is our primary frontend framework
Backend experience with Node.js and Express.js
Database experience with PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MongoDB
Comfortable with Git, Docker, and basic CI/CD workflows
Hands-on experience with AI coding tools (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, or similar)
Strong debugging skills — you can track down and fix problems independently
Reliable and consistent — you follow through on what you commit to
Communicates openly and directly — no guessing games on status or blockers
whoYouAre: Sincere — you mean what you say and say what you mean, no performance
Committed — when you take something on, you see it through without being chased
Punctual and dependable — the team can count on you to show up and follow through
Serious about your work — you care about the quality of what you put your name on
A team player — you share context, support others, and don't work in isolation
A critical thinker — you question assumptions, spot problems early, and think before acting
Professional — you handle feedback well, communicate with respect, and stay composed under pressure
Honest — if something is blocked, unclear, or going wrong, you say so immediately
niceToHave: Experience in a product-focused or agency development environment
Exposure to multiple database technologies
Experience working on live production systems with real users
Familiarity with prompt engineering for development workflows
successMetrics: Features shipped on time and without recurring post-ship issues
Low bug rate and minimal production incidents from your work
Code that fits cleanly into the existing system without creating new problems
The team always knows where your work stands
Growing scope and trust over time
whyJoin: Real ownership over what you build — not just executing tickets
On-site team environment with direct collaboration
Work on diverse software and AI-powered products across industries
Stable, long-term full-time role with room to grow
Team that values reliability and commitment over politics
